mnemonics.title: NUPC-DCF Remember Constraint Order
mnemonics.content: N - NOT NULL, U - UNIQUE, P - PRIMARY KEY, C - CHECK, D - DEFAULT, C - CONSTRAINT, F - FOREIGN KEY. Use this sequence when defining multiple constraints.

mnemonics.title: PRIMARY KEY Remember
mnemonics.content: PRIMARY KEY = Pillar of the table - One per table, can't be NULL, uniquely identifies rows. Think of it as the main door - there's only one main entrance.

mnemonics.title: FOREIGN KEY Remember
mnemonics.content: FOREIGN KEY = Foreign diplomat must respect host country's rules. The foreign key must reference an existing primary key or be NULL (diplomat not present).

acronyms.usage: Uniquely identifies each row in a table, cannot be NULL
Links one table to another, enforces referential integrity
Validates data against a specific condition
Ensures column must have a value
Ensures all values in column are different
SQL commands like CREATE, ALTER, DROP used to define constraints

visualTips.visualization: Draw a table with a key icon on PRIMARY KEY column. Show UNIQUE allowing NULL as an empty box (null = unknown = can exist once)
Draw two tables with an arrow from child table FK pointing to parent table PK. Show parent-child relationship visually like a family tree.
Draw a funnel/filter where only values satisfying the condition pass through. Values failing the check get rejected.
Show column-level constraints written next to the column, table-level constraints written after all columns in the table.

analogies.analogy: Like a required field on a job application - you cannot submit without filling it.
Like a fingerprint - each person has unique fingerprints, but you can have missing fingers (NULL).
Like a library book barcode - each book (child) must have a valid member ID (parent) to be borrowed.
Like a bouncer at a club checking if you meet the age requirement before letting in.
Like a default signature on a legal document - used when no specific signature is provided.
Like your Aadhaar/Social Security number - uniquely identifies you, cannot be shared, must exist.
